title: Neorautanenia pseudopachyrrhiza
intro: Neorautanenia pseudopachyrrhiza, commonly known as wild garlic or snake root, is a perennial plant belonging to the legume family, Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in African folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Neorautanenia pseudopachyrrhiza include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infused in hot water to make a tea, or used in the form of a powder or capsule, which is said to aid in the treatment of various health conditions.
taxonomy: Neorautanenia pseudopachyrrhiza is a plant species classified in the family Fabaceae.
